filters.notes: Zone map eliminates ~98.1% of 600 blocks. For INTERIOR blocks (zone.mn >= 8766 && zone.mx < 9131), ALL rows pass — do NOT load or check l_shipdate. For BOUNDARY blocks only (zone.mn < 8766 || zone.mx >= 9131), apply the per-row shipdate check.
Applied in BOTH interior and boundary inner loops. int8_t comparison, no decode needed.
Applied in BOTH interior and boundary inner loops. int8_t comparison, no decode needed.

layout.entries: ZoneEntry[num_blocks] where ZoneEntry = { int32_t min_date; int32_t max_date; } (8B each)

indexes.usage: Load entire zone map at startup (fits in L1 cache). For each qualifying block b: if (zones[b].mx < LO || zones[b].mn >= HI) skip. Then classify: if (zones[b].mn >= LO && zones[b].mx < HI) => interior block (dispatch fast inner loop, no shipdate load); else => boundary block (dispatch full inner loop with shipdate check).

data_structures.thread_accumulator: double rev_local (stack variable per thread, no atomics)
data_structures.final_reduction: sequential sum of nthreads thread-local doubles

inner_loop.description: Two specialized inner loop variants selected per block

interior_loop.notes: l_shipdate NOT loaded or checked — zone guarantees all rows pass. Compiler can vectorize int8 checks across rows more aggressively without the int32 outlier column.

boundary_loop.condition: zones[b].mn < LO || zones[b].mx < HI (block straddles a boundary)
boundary_loop.notes: Full three-filter check. Shipdate checked first because at block boundaries it is genuinely selective (filters rows before/after 1994 window). Original iter_0 filter order preserved.

aggregation.notes: Each thread accumulates into a stack-local double. Reduction after join is a sequential sum over nthreads partial values.

parallelism.notes: Divide qualifying row range evenly across hardware_concurrency() threads. Each thread determines its block range and dispatches interior vs boundary inner loop per block. No madvise calls of any kind — rely on OS page cache for hot runs.

optimization_notes: Root cause is Category C (work elimination): iter_0 applies a uniform three-filter inner loop to ALL qualifying blocks, including interior blocks where the zone map already guarantees every row passes the l_shipdate predicate. For the ~10 interior blocks (~83% of qualifying rows, ~1M rows), the code needlessly loads 4 bytes of l_shipdate per row and evaluates a branch that always succeeds, wasting ~4 MB of column reads and ~1M redundant branches. The fix is to split the per-block inner loop into two specialized variants: an interior loop (for blocks where zone.mn >= LO && zone.mx < HI) that reads only l_discount and l_quantity and skips l_shipdate entirely, and a boundary loop (for the ~2 blocks straddling 1994 date boundaries) that applies the full three-filter check in the original shipdate-first order. This directly eliminates the wasted work and also gives the compiler a cleaner int8-only hot path to vectorize. Deliberately avoids all changes from iter_1 (no MADV calls, no filter reordering in boundary loop, no cache-line padding on partial[]) to prevent re-introducing the regression.
